text:
Once upon a time, there was a garden gnome who traveled. He didn't like staying in one place. Every month or so he'd hitch a ride on a leaf to go visit his friends.

One autumn day, he decided to visit London. He boarded a fallen leaf and rode across leaf-strewn streets. After a couple of hours, he bumped into a squirrel.

"Squirrel!" the gnome exclaimed. "Where would you be going?"

"Weaving!"

"What fun! Do you weave baskets?"

"No," chuckled the squirrel. "I weave nuts."

"You weave nuts!"

"Aye!"

So, they rolled together until they landed beside a tall tree. The squirrel grabbed his axe and started cutting branches. With each branch chopped down, the gnome jumped higher.

Eventually, the gnome found himself perched on a branch as wide as a bus. "Thankyou!" he cried out joyously.

"Why thank you!" said the squirrel as he piled up some fallen branches.
